creator: tomek@cedro.info
text:
Thank you for quick response :-)

1. Yes I am running Xorg session with Enlightenment WM but behavior is exactly the same in bare terminal before I start X by hand. So the WM does not seem to interfere here.

2. polkit daemon is launched by pcscd. I also have dbus running system wide.

3. I have tried to modify the polkit policy and rules files to enable all pcsc to everyone but without effect (as presented in Comment 3) . I have killed both pcscd and polkit daemon just to make sure files are re-read. Either polkit does not read the files and does not apply the policy or there is other problem in pcscd 2.3.1 that prevents polkit from proper operation.

4. I have also tried to rebuild recent pcsc-lite 2.3.1 port with polkit disabled and it did not work either. Some debugging seems unavoidable (src/auth.c:148?). I spent several hours on that "black box" approach and did not find a way to impact the configuration.

5. Maybe 2.3.1 has some new access / process related stuff implemented (get_kinfo_proc() ?) and this may not be even polkit related as creating full open policy configuration does not seem to apply. The pcscd daemon runs as root so it should not be impacted by some systctl wide system restrictions (setup default hardening)? See the error message it complains "No such process" while PID matches existing working process (i.e. gpg-agent or yubikey-agent):

00000000 ../src/auth.c:148:IsClientAuthorized() Error in authorization: GDBus.Error:org.freedesktop.PolicyKit1.Error.Failed: get_kinfo_proc() failed for pid 29033: No such process

5. For now I have reverted to 2.3.0 from the pkg cache and this is a quick fix.

Thanks :-)
